2604.18913.title: LogosKG: Hardware-Optimized Scalable and Interpretable Knowledge Graph Retrieval
2604.18913.paper_summary: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are increasingly integrated with large language models (LLMs) to provide structured, verifiable reasoning. A core operation in this integration is multi-hop retrieval, yet existing systems struggle to balance efficiency, scalability, and interpretability. We introduce LogosKG, a novel, hardware-aligned framework that enables scalable and interpretable k-hop retrieval on large KGs by building on symbolic KG formulations and executing traversal as hardware-efficient operations over decomposed subject, object, and relation representations. To scale to billion-edge graphs, LogosKG integrates degree-aware partitioning, cross-graph routing, and on-demand caching. Experiments show substantial efficiency gains over CPU and GPU baselines without loss of retrieval fidelity. With proven performance in KG retrieval, a downstream two-round KG-LLM interaction demonstrates how LogosKG enables large-scale, evidence-grounded analysis of how KG topology, such as hop distribution and connectivity, shapes the alignment between structured biomedical knowledge and LLM diagnostic reasoning, thereby opening the door for next-generation KG-LLM integration. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/LARK-NLP-Lab/LogosKG, and an online demo is available at https://lark-nlp-lab-logoskg.hf.space/.
2604.18913.paper_summary_zh: 知識圖譜（KGs）越來越多地與大型語言模型（LLMs）整合，以提供結構化、可驗證的推理。這種整合中的核心操作是多跳檢索，但現有系統在效率、可擴展性和可解釋性之間難以取得平衡。我們介紹了LogosKG，一個新穎的硬體對齊框架，通過建立在符號KG公式之上，並將遍歷作為針對分解的主體、對象和關係表示的硬體高效操作來實現可擴展和可解釋的k-hop檢索。為了擴展到十億邊的圖，LogosKG整合了度感知分區、跨圖路由和按需緩存。實驗顯示，相較於CPU和GPU基準，效率有顯著提升，而檢索的準確性沒有損失。通過在KG檢索中證明的性能，一個下游的兩輪KG-LLM互動展示了LogosKG如何促進大規模、基於證據的分析，說明KG拓撲（例如跳數分佈和連通性）如何塑造結構化生物醫學知識與LLM診斷推理之間的對齊，從而為下一代KG-LLM整合打開了大門。源代碼可在 https://github.com/LARK-NLP-Lab/LogosKG 上公開獲得，線上演示可在 https://lark-nlp-lab-logoskg.hf.space/ 獲得。
2604.18913.author: He Cheng et.al.
2604.18913.authors: He Cheng, Yifu Wu, Saksham Khatwani, Maya Kruse, Dmitriy Dligach, Timothy A. Miller, Majid Afshar, Yanjun Gao
